questions.question: Who is the poet of 'Paper Boats'?
questions.explanation: Tagore, a Nobel laureate, wrote this poem to capture the innocent imagination of childhood.

questions.question: What does the child write on the paper boats?
questions.correct_answer: His name and the name of his village
questions.explanation: He hopes that someone in a distant land will find the boats and know who he is.

questions.question: What kind of flowers does the child put in the boats?
questions.explanation: He gathers these morning flowers and hopes they will be carried safely to land in the night.

questions.question: What does the child see in the sky while launching his boats?
questions.correct_answer: Little clouds setting their white bulging sails
questions.explanation: The child imagines the clouds are also boats in the sky, racing against his own.

questions.question: Who does the child imagine is sailing in the clouds?
questions.explanation: He believes a friend in the heavens sends the clouds down to race with his paper boats.

questions.question: What happens when night falls in the poem?
questions.correct_answer: The child dreams that his boats are still floating under the stars
questions.explanation: The 'fairies of sleep' are said to be sailing in them, with baskets full of dreams.

questions.question: Why does the child float paper boats one by one?
questions.correct_answer: To see them float down the running stream
questions.explanation: It is a daily ritual that connects the child's small world to the vast, unknown world outside.

questions.question: What is the primary theme of the poem?
questions.explanation: The poem reflects the simple joy of play and the desire to be known by others.

questions.question: What does the 'running stream' symbolize?
questions.correct_answer: The flow of time and the journey of life
questions.explanation: The stream carries the child's message far away, symbolizing the bridge between the known and unknown.

questions.question: What is the tone of 'Paper Boats'?
questions.explanation: The poem uses soft imagery to create a peaceful and imaginative atmosphere.

notes.title: A Child’s Hope
notes.content: The act of writing his name shows a universal human desire to connect with others beyond his immediate surroundings.

notes.title: Nature as a Playmate
notes.content: The child interacts with the stream, the flowers, and the clouds as if they are active participants in his game.

notes.title: Imagery of Shiuli Flowers
notes.content: Shiuli flowers (Nyctanthes arbor-tristis) bloom at night and fall in the early morning, adding a sense of freshness and purity.

notes.title: The World of Dreams
notes.content: The poem transitions from the physical world of the stream to the magical world of fairies and dreams at night.

notes.title: Tagore’s Style
notes.content: Simple language is used to convey deep emotional truths about human nature and wonder.

facts.fact: The poem is part of Tagore's famous collection titled 'The Crescent Moon'.
Paper boats are a traditional and simple toy used by children across India during the monsoon season.
The poem highlights the contrast between the small, fragile boat and the vast, powerful stream.
